req.title: Consent management (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
pytest test suite for Patient Consent Management Requirements
Requirement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing;
             revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes.
             Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
"""

import pytest
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs – replace with real imports in production
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ConsentStatus(str, Enum):
    GRANTED = "GRANTED"
    REVOKED = "REVOKED"
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    EXPIRED = "EXPIRED"


@dataclass
class ConsentRecord:
    patient_id: str
    status: ConsentStatus
    timestamp: datetime
    consent_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    granted_by: Optional[str] = None
    revoked_by: Optional[str] = None
    scope: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    expiry: Optional[datetime] = None


class ConsentError(Exception):
    pass


class ConsentService:
    """
    Stub – mirrors the interface expected from the real implementation.
    Replace method bodies with real calls during integration.
    """

    ENFORCEMENT_DEADLINE_SECONDS: int = 900  # 15 minutes

    def __init__(self, repository, enforcement_engine, audit_log):
        self._repo = repository
        self._engine = enforcement_engine
        self._audit = audit_log

    def grant_consent(self, patient_id: str, scope: list[str],
                      granted_by: str,
                      expiry: Optional[datetime] = None) -> ConsentRecord:
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id=patient_id,
            status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            granted_by=granted_by,
            scope=scope,
            expiry=expiry,
        )
        self._repo.save(record)
        self._audit.log("GRANT", record)
        return record

    def revoke_consent(self, patient_id: str, revoked_by: str) -> ConsentRecord:
        record = self._repo.get_active(patient_id)
        if record is None:
            raise ConsentError(f"No active consent for patient {patient_id}")
        record.status = ConsentStatus.REVOKED
        record.revoked_by = revoked_by
        record.timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._repo.save(record)
        self._engine.enforce_revocation(record)
        self._audit.log("REVOKE", record)
        return record

    def is_data_sharing_permitted(self, patient_id: str) -> bool:
        record = self._repo.get_active(patient_id)
        if record is None:
            return False
        if record.status != ConsentStatus.GRANTED:
            return False
        if record.expiry and datetime.now(timezone.utc) > record.expiry:
            return False
        return True

    def get_enforcement_deadline(self, revocation_timestamp: datetime) -> datetime:
        return revocation_timestamp + timedelta(
            seconds=self.ENFORCEMENT_DEADLINE_SECONDS
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_active.return_value = None
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_engine():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def service(mock_repo, mock_engine, mock_audit):
    return ConsentService(mock_repo, mock_engine, mock_audit)


@pytest.fixture
def granted_record():
    return ConsentRecord(
        patient_id="P-001",
        status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
        timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        granted_by="patient",
        scope=["research", "treatment"],
    )


@pytest.fixture
def now_utc():
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Grant Consent – Happy Path
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestGrantConsent:

    def test_grant_returns_granted_status(self, service, mock_repo):
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        assert record.status == ConsentStatus.GRANTED

    def test_grant_stores_record_in_repository(self, service, mock_repo):
        service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_grant_records_audit_entry(self, service, mock_audit):
        service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once_with("GRANT", pytest.approx(
            service, abs=0  # any ConsentRecord
        ))
        event, _ = mock_audit.log.call_args[0]
        assert event == "GRANT"

    def test_grant_assigns_unique_consent_id(self, service):
        r1 = service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        r2 = service.grant_consent("P-002", ["research"], "patient")
        assert r1.consent_id != r2.consent_id

    def test_grant_stores_patient_id(self, service):
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        assert record.patient_id == "P-001"

    def test_grant_stores_scope(self, service):
        scope = ["treatment", "research", "billing"]
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", scope, "patient")
        assert record.scope == scope

    def test_grant_stores_empty_scope(self, service):
        """Boundary: empty scope list is valid (minimal consent)."""
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        assert record.scope == []

    def test_grant_timestamp_is_utc_aware(self, service, now_utc):
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        assert record.timestamp.tzinfo is not None
        assert record.timestamp >= now_utc

    def test_grant_with_future_expiry(self, service):
        expiry = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=365)
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient",
                                       expiry=expiry)
        assert record.expiry == expiry

    def test_grant_with_no_expiry(self, service):
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", ["treatment"], "patient")
        assert record.expiry is None

    def test_grant_data_sharing_permitted_after_grant(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is True

    def test_grant_permitted_for_different_patients_independently(
            self, service, mock_repo):
        record_p1 = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record_p1
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is True

        record_p2 = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-002", status=ConsentStatus.REVOKED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record_p2
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-002") is False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Revoke Consent – Happy Path
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestRevokeConsent:

    def test_revoke_returns_revoked_status(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        record = service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        assert record.status == ConsentStatus.REVOKED

    def test_revoke_stores_updated_record(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved_record = mock_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_record.status == ConsentStatus.REVOKED

    def test_revoke_triggers_enforcement_engine(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        mock_engine.enforce_revocation.assert_called_once()

    def test_revoke_records_audit_entry(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        event, _ = mock_audit.log.call_args[0]
        assert event == "REVOKE"

    def test_revoke_stores_revoked_by(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        record = service.revoke_consent("P-001", "admin")
        assert record.revoked_by == "admin"

    def test_revoke_updates_timestamp(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record, now_utc):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        record = service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        assert record.timestamp >= now_utc

    def test_revoke_without_existing_consent_raises_error(
            self, service, mock_repo):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = None
        with pytest.raises(ConsentError):
            service.revoke_consent("P-UNKNOWN", "patient")

    def test_revoke_prevents_data_sharing(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        # After revocation, the repo should return the revoked record
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record  # now REVOKED
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is False

    def test_revoke_idempotent_second_revoke_raises_error(
            self, service, mock_repo):
        """Second revoke attempt when no active consent exists."""
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = None
        with pytest.raises(ConsentError):
            service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Enforcement Deadline – 15-Minute Boundary Tests (Core SLA)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS = 900  # 15 minutes × 60


class TestEnforcementDeadline:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("offset_seconds,expected_label", [
        (0,           "exactly at revocation"),
        (1,           "1 second after revocation"),
        (449,         "just under 7.5 minutes"),
        (450,         "exactly 7.5 minutes (midpoint)"),
        (899,         "1 second before deadline"),
        (900,         "exactly at 15-minute deadline"),
    ])
    def test_enforcement_deadline_within_window(
            self, service, offset_seconds, expected_label):
        """Revocation timestamp + offset must remain ≤ deadline."""
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        enforcement_ts = revocation_ts + timedelta(seconds=offset_seconds)
        assert enforcement_ts <= deadline, (
            f"Enforcement not within 15-min SLA ({expected_label})"
        )

    def test_enforcement_deadline_is_exactly_15_minutes_after_revocation(
            self, service):
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        delta = (deadline - revocation_ts).total_seconds()
        assert delta == pytest.approx(ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS, abs=0.001)

    def test_enforcement_deadline_exceeded_by_one_second(self, service):
        """One second past the deadline is a violation."""
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        enforcement_ts = revocation_ts + timedelta(
            seconds=ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS + 1
        )
        assert enforcement_ts > deadline  # confirms test intent

    def test_enforcement_engine_called_immediately_on_revoke(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine, granted_record, now_utc):
        """Enforcement engine must be invoked synchronously / immediately."""
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        call_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        mock_engine.enforce_revocation.assert_called_once()
        # Verify call happened within a 2-second window of the revocation
        record_ts = mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_args[0][0].timestamp
        assert (call_time - record_ts).total_seconds() < 2

    def test_enforcement_deadline_with_epoch_timestamp(self, service):
        """Boundary: Unix epoch as revocation timestamp."""
        epoch = datetime(1970, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(epoch)
        assert deadline == epoch + timedelta(seconds=ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS)

    def test_enforcement_deadline_at_end_of_day(self, service):
        """Boundary: revocation 1 second before midnight crosses date boundary."""
        end_of_day = datetime(2024, 3, 31, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(end_of_day)
        expected = datetime(2024, 4, 1, 0, 14, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        assert deadline == expected

    def test_enforcement_deadline_at_leap_second_boundary(self, service):
        """Boundary: revocation during last minute of a leap-second day."""
        leap_day = datetime(2024, 2, 29, 23, 45, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(leap_day)
        assert deadline == leap_day + timedelta(seconds=ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS)

    def test_enforcement_deadline_year_boundary(self, service):
        """Boundary: revocation at year rollover."""
        new_years_eve = datetime(2023, 12, 31, 23, 55, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(new_years_eve)
        assert deadline.year == 2024

    def test_enforcement_deadline_microsecond_precision(self, service):
        """Boundary: timestamp with microseconds."""
        ts = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 12, 0, 0, 999999, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(ts)
        assert deadline.microsecond == 999999

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("minutes", [0, 1, 7, 14, 15])
    def test_enforcement_deadline_parametrized_minutes(
            self, service, minutes):
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        check_ts = revocation_ts + timedelta(minutes=minutes)
        if minutes <= 15:
            assert check_ts <= deadline
        else:
            assert check_ts > deadline


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Data Sharing Permission Logic
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestDataSharingPermission:

    def test_no_consent_record_denies_sharing(self, service, mock_repo):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = None
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-NEW") is False

    def test_pending_status_denies_sharing(self, service, mock_repo):
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.PENDING,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is False

    def test_expired_status_denies_sharing(self, service, mock_repo):
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.EXPIRED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is False

    def test_revoked_status_denies_sharing(self, service, mock_repo):
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.REVOKED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is False

    def test_granted_status_permits_sharing(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is True

    def test_granted_with_past_expiry_denies_sharing(
            self, service, mock_repo):
        """Boundary: consent expired 1 second ago."""
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            scope=[],
            expiry=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1))
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is False

    def test_granted_with_expiry_in_future_permits_sharing(
            self, service, mock_repo):
        """Boundary: consent expires in 1 second – still valid."""
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            scope=[],
            expiry=datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=1))
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is True

    def test_granted_expiry_exactly_now_denies_sharing(
            self, service, mock_repo):
        """Boundary: expiry == now (expired at this exact microsecond)."""
        exact_now = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(microseconds=1)
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=exact_now, scope=[], expiry=exact_now)
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = record
        assert service.is_data_sharing_permitted("P-001") is False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Timestamp Boundary Tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestTimestampBoundaries:

    def test_grant_timestamp_not_in_future(self, service):
        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        assert before <= record.timestamp <= after

    def test_revoke_timestamp_not_in_future(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        record = service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        assert before <= record.timestamp <= after

    def test_revoke_timestamp_not_before_grant_timestamp(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        grant_ts = granted_record.timestamp
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        record = service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        assert record.timestamp >= grant_ts

    def test_enforcement_deadline_uses_revocation_timestamp_not_grant(
            self, service):
        grant_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=2)
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        # Deadline must be based on revocation, not grant
        assert deadline == revocation_ts + timedelta(seconds=ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS)
        assert deadline != grant_ts + timedelta(seconds=ENFORCEMENT_SECONDS)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("future_offset_days", [1, 30, 365, 3650])
    def test_future_expiry_dates_are_accepted(self, service, future_offset_days):
        expiry = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=future_offset_days)
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient", expiry=expiry)
        assert record.expiry == expiry

    def test_past_expiry_date_can_be_stored(self, service):
        """
        The service layer may accept past expiry (validation might be
        elsewhere); the resulting record is stored. Data-sharing check
        enforces the expiry separately.
        """
        past_expiry = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient",
                                       expiry=past_expiry)
        assert record.expiry == past_expiry


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Numeric Boundary Tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestNumericBoundaries:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("patient_id", [
        "P-0",           # minimal-length numeric suffix
        "P-" + "9" * 20, # very long numeric suffix
        "P-000000001",   # leading zeros
        "P-2147483647",  # INT32 max
        "P-9999999999",  # large 10-digit number
    ])
    def test_grant_consent_various_patient_ids(self, service, patient_id):
        record = service.grant_consent(patient_id, [], "patient")
        assert record.patient_id == patient_id

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("scope_size", [0, 1, 10, 100, 1000])
    def test_grant_consent_scope_size_boundaries(self, service, scope_size):
        scope = [f"scope_{i}" for i in range(scope_size)]
        record = service.grant_consent("P-001", scope, "patient")
        assert len(record.scope) == scope_size

    def test_enforcement_deadline_constant_is_exactly_900_seconds(
            self, service):
        assert service.ENFORCEMENT_DEADLINE_SECONDS == 900

    def test_enforcement_deadline_is_not_899_seconds(self, service):
        """Boundary below SLA."""
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        under_limit = revocation_ts + timedelta(seconds=899)
        assert under_limit < deadline  # 899 s < 900 s deadline

    def test_enforcement_deadline_is_not_901_seconds(self, service):
        """Boundary above SLA."""
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        over_limit = revocation_ts + timedelta(seconds=901)
        assert over_limit > deadline  # 901 s > 900 s deadline

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("seconds", [0, 1, 899, 900])
    def test_various_seconds_within_or_at_deadline(self, service, seconds):
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        check = revocation_ts + timedelta(seconds=seconds)
        assert check <= deadline

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("seconds", [901, 902, 1800, 86400])
    def test_various_seconds_beyond_deadline(self, service, seconds):
        revocation_ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        deadline = service.get_enforcement_deadline(revocation_ts)
        check = revocation_ts + timedelta(seconds=seconds)
        assert check > deadline


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Audit Trail Completeness
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuditTrail:

    def test_grant_logs_correct_patient_id(self, service, mock_audit):
        service.grant_consent("P-999", [], "patient")
        _, record = mock_audit.log.call_args[0]
        assert record.patient_id == "P-999"

    def test_revoke_logs_correct_patient_id(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        _, record = mock_audit.log.call_args[0]
        assert record.patient_id == "P-001"

    def test_grant_then_revoke_produces_two_audit_entries(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit, granted_record):
        # Grant
        service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        # Revoke
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        assert mock_audit.log.call_count == 2
        events = [c[0][0] for c in mock_audit.log.call_args_list]
        assert events == ["GRANT", "REVOKE"]

    def test_audit_log_contains_timestamp(self, service, mock_audit):
        service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        _, record = mock_audit.log.call_args[0]
        assert isinstance(record.timestamp, datetime)

    def test_audit_log_contains_consent_id(self, service, mock_audit):
        service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        _, record = mock_audit.log.call_args[0]
        assert record.consent_id is not None
        assert len(record.consent_id) > 0


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Enforcement Engine Integration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestEnforcementEngineIntegration:

    def test_enforcement_engine_receives_correct_patient_id(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        enforced_record = mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_args[0][0]
        assert enforced_record.patient_id == "P-001"

    def test_enforcement_engine_receives_revoked_status(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        enforced_record = mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_args[0][0]
        assert enforced_record.status == ConsentStatus.REVOKED

    def test_enforcement_engine_not_called_on_grant(
            self, service, mock_engine):
        service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        mock_engine.enforce_revocation.assert_not_called()

    def test_enforcement_engine_called_once_per_revocation(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine, granted_record):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        assert mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_count == 1

    def test_enforcement_engine_receives_revocation_timestamp(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine, granted_record, now_utc):
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        enforced_record = mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_args[0][0]
        assert enforced_record.timestamp >= now_utc


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Concurrency / Race Condition Stubs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestConcurrencyScenarios:

    def test_simultaneous_grant_and_revoke_last_write_wins(
            self, service, mock_repo, granted_record):
        """
        Verify the repository is called for both operations.
        Real concurrency control belongs in the repository layer.
        """
        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = granted_record
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")
        # Simulate a second grant arriving after revoke
        service.grant_consent("P-001", [], "patient")
        assert mock_repo.save.call_count == 2

    def test_multiple_revocations_for_different_patients(
            self, service, mock_repo, mock_engine):
        p1 = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-001", status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])
        p2 = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id="P-002", status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc), scope=[])

        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = p1
        service.revoke_consent("P-001", "patient")

        mock_repo.get_active.return_value = p2
        service.revoke_consent("P-002", "patient")

        assert mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_count == 2
        patients_enforced = {
            c[0][0].patient_id
            for c in mock_engine.enforce_revocation.call_args_list
        }
        assert patients_enforced == {"P-001", "P-002"}
```

---

## Design Rationale

| Area | Decisions |
|---|---|
| **Structure** | Nine `class`-based test groups, one per concern, making failures easy to isolate in CI output |
| **Stubs vs mocks** | A thin `ConsentService` stub with a clear interface; `mock_repo`, `mock_engine`,

"""
pytest test suite for Patient Consent Management Requirements
Requirement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing;
             revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes.
             Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
"""

import pytest
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs – replace with real imports in production
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ConsentStatus(str, Enum):
    GRANTED = "GRANTED"
    REVOKED = "REVOKED"
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    EXPIRED = "EXPIRED"


@dataclass
class ConsentRecord:
    patient_id: str
    status: ConsentStatus
    timestamp: datetime
    consent_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    granted_by: Optional[str] = None
    revoked_by: Optional[str] = None
    scope: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    expiry: Optional[datetime] = None


class ConsentError(Exception):
    pass


class ConsentService:
    """
    Stub – mirrors the interface expected from the real implementation.
    Replace method bodies with real calls during integration.
    """

    ENFORCEMENT_DEADLINE_SECONDS: int = 900  # 15 minutes

    def __init__(self, repository, enforcement_engine, audit_log):
        self._repo = repository
        self._engine = enforcement_engine
        self._audit = audit_log

    def grant_consent(self, patient_id: str, scope: list[str],
                      granted_by: str,
                      expiry: Optional[datetime] = None) -> ConsentRecord:
        record = ConsentRecord(
            patient_id=patient_id,
            status=ConsentStatus.GRANTED,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            granted_by=granted_by,
            scope=scope,
            expiry=expiry,
        )
        self._repo.save(record)
        self._audit.log("GRANT", record)
        return record

    def revoke_consent(self, patient_id: str, revoked_by: str) -> ConsentRecord:
        record = self._repo.get_active(patient_id)
        if record is None:
            raise ConsentError(f"No active consent for patient {patient_id}")
        record.status = ConsentStatus.REVOKED
        record.revoked_by = revoked_by
        record.timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        self._repo.save(record)
        self._engine.enforce_revocation(record)
        self._audit.log("REVOKE", record)
        return record

    def is_data_sharing_permitted(self, patient_id: str) -> bool:
        record = self._repo.get_active(patient_id)
        if record is None:
            return False
        if record.status != ConsentStatus.GRANTED:
            return False
        if record.expiry and datetime.now(timezone.utc) > record.expiry:
            return False
        return True

    def get_enforcement_deadline(self, revocation_timestamp: datetime) -> datetime:
        return revocation_timestamp + timedelta(
            seconds=self.ENFORCEMENT_DEADLINE_SECONDS
        )

# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
